broad_catches.reason: Health diagnostics must degrade to partial snapshots when optional provider, metrics, event-store, or profile probes fail.
Log tail reads are operator diagnostics and return a bounded 500 response instead of crashing route handling.
Legacy-route telemetry must not break compatibility routing when metrics are unavailable.
Direct fallback route registration logs unexpected router-add failures without blocking PromptServer registration.
Runtime profile probing is best-effort today; future work should pin profile import/runtime failures more explicitly.
MAE posture validation is a startup diagnostic gate and logs unavailability without preventing core local loading.
Connector unit tests may stub the import graph; sanitizer import fallback keeps tests and standalone connector loading deterministic.
Connector command dispatch is an external-message boundary and converts handler failures into a non-sensitive operator response.
Template metadata lookup is best-effort; command execution falls back to explicit prompt input names when metadata is unavailable.
Plugin/shutdown hook registration is currently non-fatal but should gain narrower plugin failure coverage.
Bootstrap initialization logs context and re-raises so security gate failures remain fail-closed.
Background route retry is a PromptServer warm-up boundary and keeps retrying after transient registration failures.
Initial route registration logs context and re-raises so unexpected bootstrap failures are visible and fail-closed.
